findings.title: Add Notification Priority Levels
findings.description: Implement a three-tier priority system for notifications (urgent, normal, low) to help users distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates.
assumptions: Users need to differentiate between notification types to avoid missing important messages.

evidence.excerpt: Users need a way to distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates.

recommendations: Design a visual differentiation for each priority level.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user receives notifications WHEN they are sent THEN the notifications should display with the correct priority level and visual indicators.

findings.title: Fix Notification Delivery on Android 14
findings.description: Resolve the issue where push notifications fail to deliver on Android 14 devices, affecting approximately 8% of users.
assumptions: The issue is related to FCM token refresh and user permissions.

evidence.excerpt: Several users on Android 14 report that push notifications silently fail.

recommendations: Investigate FCM token handling and user permission settings.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user on Android 14 WHEN they receive a notification THEN the notification should be delivered successfully without requiring manual re-enabling of permissions.

findings.title: Implement ML-based Notification Batching
findings.description: Develop a machine learning model to batch low-priority notifications into a digest delivered at optimal times based on user engagement patterns.
assumptions: Users prefer receiving notifications in batches rather than individually.

evidence.excerpt: Batch low-priority notifications into a digest delivered at optimal times.

recommendations: Conduct user testing to validate batching preferences.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user has multiple low-priority notifications WHEN the ML model processes them THEN they should be batched and delivered at the optimal time.

findings.title: Add User Preference Page for Notification Categories
findings.description: Create a settings page that allows users to opt in/out of specific notification categories (marketing, product updates, social, transactional).
assumptions: Users want control over the types of notifications they receive.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

recommendations: Ensure the new preferences API respects existing global mute settings.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the notification preferences page WHEN they select categories THEN their preferences should be saved and respected in future notifications.

findings.title: Reduce Notification Delivery Latency
findings.description: Improve notification delivery latency from the 95th percentile of 340ms to under 200ms by optimizing the delivery pipeline.
assumptions: Synchronous database writes are causing delays.

evidence.excerpt: Performance monitoring shows p95 notification delivery latency at 340ms.

recommendations: Implement async writes and batch inserts for analytics events.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a notification is sent WHEN measured THEN the delivery latency should be under 200ms for 95% of notifications.

findings.title: Conduct Experiment on Notification Content Optimization
findings.description: Run experiments to test different notification content strategies to improve click-through rates and reduce unsubscribe rates.
assumptions: Different messaging strategies will impact user engagement.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct experiments to test different notification content strategies.

recommendations: Include A/B testing of messaging and timing.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN different notification content strategies are tested WHEN users interact with notifications THEN the click-through rates and unsubscribe rates should be measured and analyzed.

findings.title: Address Accessibility Concerns in Notification Center
findings.description: Improve the navigation of the notification center for users with accessibility needs, particularly for screen reader users and colorblind users.
assumptions: Accessibility improvements will enhance user experience for diverse users.

evidence.excerpt: Users with accessibility needs face challenges navigating the notification center.

recommendations: Conduct accessibility testing with users who have disabilities.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user with accessibility needs accesses the notification center WHEN they navigate THEN they should be able to do so without barriers and receive all notifications.

findings.title: Gather User Feedback on Notification Preferences
findings.description: Implement a feedback mechanism to collect user insights on notification preferences and delivery timing.
assumptions: User feedback will provide valuable insights for future enhancements.

evidence.excerpt: There is currently insufficient data on user preferences for notification timing.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the feedback form WHEN they submit their preferences THEN their feedback should be recorded and analyzed for future improvements.

findings.title: Monitor Notification Delivery Metrics
findings.description: Set up monitoring and alerting for notification delivery metrics to ensure compliance with performance goals.
assumptions: Monitoring will help maintain performance standards.

evidence.excerpt: The 95th Percentile Delivery Latency metric measures the time taken to deliver notifications.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN monitoring is set up WHEN delivery metrics are collected THEN alerts should trigger if performance goals are not met.

findings.title: Create Documentation for Notification Features
findings.description: Develop comprehensive documentation for all new notification features, including user guides and API references.
assumptions: Documentation will improve user understanding and adoption.

evidence.excerpt: This requires a new preferences API endpoint and a React settings component.

recommendations: Ensure documentation is user-friendly and accessible.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN new features are implemented WHEN documentation is created THEN it should be clear, comprehensive, and accessible to all users.

summary: The analysis has led to the identification of 10 key requirements for SmartNotify, focusing on enhancing notification prioritization, reliability, user preferences, and accessibility. Each requirement is structured with clear acceptance criteria, priorities, and phases to guide development efforts effectively.
